prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Ah, me hearty, ye be wonderin' why the sky appears as a vast ocean o' blue, do ye? Well, sit ye down, and let me spin ye a tale. Ye see, the light from the great ball o' fire in the sky, the sun, it be shinin' bright and true. When it hits the vast expanse o' air 'round our good Earth, it starts a dance, a right playful one it is.

Now, most o' the colors in that light be scatterin' in all directions, but the blue light, it be smaller, more nimble, if ye will. So, it dances and bounces 'round the sky more than the others. This makes the blue light spread all 'round, and that's why ye see the sky as a grand canvas o' blue when ye look up. But mind ye, this be just what I reckon from many a day spent lookin' at the sky whilst sailin' the seven seas. Now, how 'bout ye help me with a drink, and we can ponder the mysteries o' the heavens over a good ale!
